intro: Acronia sanchoi, also known as Sancho's acronia, is a plant species in the family Rubiaceae, native to the Philippines. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may aid in the treatment of various ailments such as fever, cough, and skin infections. The bioactive constituents of Acronia sanchoi include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and triterpenoids, which are believed to contribute to its therapeutic properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Acronia sanchoi is often prepared as a decoction or an infusion, and can also be used in the form of a tincture or a topical cream, allowing for its bioactive compounds to be absorbed and utilized by the body.
